meta.building: Façade occidentale et tours de Notre-Dame de Paris
meta.building_en: West facade & towers of Notre-Dame de Paris
meta.build_state: The 'harmonic facade' (c.1200–1250), restored by Viollet-le-Duc & Lassus (1844–64); cleaned 1990s; post-2019-fire restoration completed 2024. The two towers were never given their planned spires.
meta.generated_by: scripts/derive-notre-dame-towers.mjs (Yingzao Rule Engine — west towers & facade)

units.note: All positions/dimensions in metres. Facade in the X–Y plane facing +Z; ground at y=0.

components.role: The harmonic-facade body below the towers — the measured 43.5 m width is the verifier's primary anchor (T01).
components.note: Width 43.5 m multiply confirmed (FRIENDS-WEST-FACADE + TECHNO-SCIENCE + BNF). Body height 45 m. Stone TYPE measured (Lutetian limestone); exact colour is conjecture/GAP — not invented.

components.name_fr: corniche de la galerie des chimères
components.role: The horizontal colonnade course (Galerie des Chimères level) linking the two towers across the facade top.
components.note: Register course form scaled off the Dehio-Bezold PD section (reconstructed_design); the gallery's exact height above ground is a GAP.

components.role: Square belfry tower culminating at the measured 69 m; flat-topped — the intended spire was never built.
components.note: Top at 69 m (MEASURED, T02). Width 14.91 m is single-source → reconstructed_design. N≠S asymmetry preserved.

components.role: Flat belfry parapet — the documented fact that the towers were never given their planned spires.
components.note: Flat top is the MEASURED fact (spires_never_built); top edge marks the 69 m culmination.

components.role: Square belfry tower culminating at the measured 69 m; flat-topped — the intended spire was never built.
components.note: Top at 69 m (MEASURED, T02). Width 13.69 m is single-source → reconstructed_design. N≠S asymmetry preserved.

components.role: Flat belfry parapet — the documented fact that the towers were never given their planned spires.
components.note: Flat top is the MEASURED fact (spires_never_built); top edge marks the 69 m culmination.

components.role: One of the four vertical buttresses dividing the facade into its tripartite bays.
components.note: Buttress count = 4 MEASURED (FRIENDS-WEST-FACADE + EN-WIKI-NDP). Thickness 2.4 m is rule_derived massing.

components.role: One of the four vertical buttresses dividing the facade into its tripartite bays.
components.note: Buttress count = 4 MEASURED (FRIENDS-WEST-FACADE + EN-WIKI-NDP). Thickness 2.4 m is rule_derived massing.

components.role: One of the four vertical buttresses dividing the facade into its tripartite bays.
components.note: Buttress count = 4 MEASURED (FRIENDS-WEST-FACADE + EN-WIKI-NDP). Thickness 2.4 m is rule_derived massing.

components.role: One of the four vertical buttresses dividing the facade into its tripartite bays.
components.note: Buttress count = 4 MEASURED (FRIENDS-WEST-FACADE + EN-WIKI-NDP). Thickness 2.4 m is rule_derived massing.

components.role: Lateral west portal at the facade base.
components.note: Per-portal width is a GAP (canonical _GAP_portal_widths_m); opening rule_derived from the bay width. Centre opening widest (FRIENDS-PORTALS confirms centre widest, gives no metric).

components.name_en: Pointed (two-centred) arch — Portal of the Virgin (north/left)
components.role: The pointed arch head struck by the two-centred-arch rule from the two springing points.
components.note: Two-centred (equilateral) Gothic arch — pure geometry struck from the springing points; rise = halfwidth × √3 (rule_derived, not surveyed).

components.name_en: Portal of the Last Judgment (centre, widest)
components.role: The central Portal of the Last Judgment — the widest of the three, per the cited record.
components.note: Per-portal width is a GAP (canonical _GAP_portal_widths_m); opening rule_derived from the bay width. Centre opening widest (FRIENDS-PORTALS confirms centre widest, gives no metric).

components.name_en: Pointed (two-centred) arch — Portal of the Last Judgment (centre, widest)
components.role: The pointed arch head struck by the two-centred-arch rule from the two springing points.
components.note: Two-centred (equilateral) Gothic arch — pure geometry struck from the springing points; rise = halfwidth × √3 (rule_derived, not surveyed).

components.role: Lateral west portal at the facade base.
components.note: Per-portal width is a GAP (canonical _GAP_portal_widths_m); opening rule_derived from the bay width. Centre opening widest (FRIENDS-PORTALS confirms centre widest, gives no metric).

components.role: The pointed arch head struck by the two-centred-arch rule from the two springing points.
components.note: Two-centred (equilateral) Gothic arch — pure geometry struck from the springing points; rise = halfwidth × √3 (rule_derived, not surveyed).

components.role: One of the 28 Kings of Judah on the Gallery of Kings — a 19th-c Viollet-le-Duc restoration (the medieval originals were decapitated in 1793).
components.note: Count 28 MEASURED (T03); figures reconstructed_design (19th-c VLD restoration, Geoffroi-Dechaume; 21 original heads at Cluny). Band height ~20 m is single-source → reconstructed_design placement, not surveyed.

components.role: Head of a Gallery-of-Kings figure (19th-c restoration; the 21 medieval heads are at the Musée de Cluny).
components.note: Head top lands at band + 3.5 m (the measured figure height). Originals at the Musée de Cluny (CLUNY-HEADS).

components.role: The outer ring of the west rose window — its 9.6 m diameter is the verifier's rose anchor (T05).
components.note: Diameter 9.6 m MEASURED (FRIENDS-ROSE-WINDOWS + TECHNO-SCIENCE + BNF). Radial geometry below is rule_derived from the cited 24-ray count.

components.role: Central hub of the radial wheel from which the spokes radiate.
components.note: Radial wheel hub — pure rose-radial geometry.

components.role: One radial spoke of the wheel tracery — radial geometry from the centre.

components.role: A representative chimera on the 19th-c Galerie des Chimères — a Viollet-le-Duc/Lassus invention; a FEW are shown, never a sourced total.
components.note: Form reconstructed_design from the PD VLD drawings; rendered count is a representative FEW flagged CONJECTURE (no reliable published total — _GAP_gargoyle_count). Never presented as a sourced count.

components.role: A projecting gargoyle waterspout (functional) — the ones seen today are largely 19th-c replacements.
components.note: Representative gargoyle (19th-c replacement). Form reconstructed_design from VLD; rendered as a flagged conjecture, never a sourced count.

components.role: Corner pinnacle on the tower parapet, set out by the 1486 Roriczer rule.
components.note: Roriczer pinnacle: base square → 45°-rotated inscribed squares (ad quadratum, 1/√2). Dimensions are a GAP (_GAP_pinnacle_dims) → proportions rule_derived, not surveyed.

components.role: Tapering cap of the tower pinnacle.
components.note: Pinnacle cap — Roriczer/ad-quadratum geometry; proportions conjecture (_GAP_pinnacle_dims).
